title: Vue Composition API Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of setup, ref, reactive, and Composables.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of the Composition API in Vue 3?
options: To author components using imported functions for maximum flexibility and logic reusability.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which hook in a component is the entry point for the Composition API in Vue 3?
When using 'ref()' to create reactive data, how do you access its actual value in JavaScript?
What is the key difference between 'ref()' and 'reactive()'?
options: ref() is for primitive types and requires .value, while reactive() is for objects and does not.
ref() is only for class components.

questions.question: Are Lifecycle Hooks (like onMounted) still available when using the Composition API?
options: Yes, as functions that can be imported and called inside setup().
No, they are only for the Options API.
